steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: Look for a number that divides both terms in $12x^2+144$. The coefficient $12$ and the constant $144$ are both divisible by $12$, so $12$ is the common factor to pull out.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content:
When $12$ is factored out, divide each original term by $12$:
$$12x^2\div 12=x^2$$
$$144\div 12=12$$
So
$$12x^2+144=12(x^2+12)$$

steps.title: Check by distributing
steps.content:
Distribute $12$ back into the parentheses to confirm the expression is unchanged:
$$12(x^2+12)=12x^2+144$$

steps.title: Select the equivalent form
steps.content: The equivalent factored expression is $12(x^2+12)$, which appears in the choices. The answer is B.
